q: The Carnot engine operates on an ideal reversible cycle between two heat reservoirs.
Real engines are less efficient than the Carnot engine because they do not operate through a reversible cycle.
In an irreversible process, the system can be returned to its initial condition along the same path on the $p-V$ diagram.
The most important condition for a process to be reversible in thermodynamics is that the process should be carried out infinitesimally slowly.
The Carnot cycle consists of four reversible processes: two isothermal processes and two adiabatic processes.
In the Carnot cycle, step 2 is defined as reversible isothermal expansion.
During the adiabatic compression step in a Carnot engine, the temperature of the gas rises to $T_{H}$.
A corollary to Carnot's theorem states that all reversible engines operating between the same heat reservoirs are equally efficient.
Heat convection is the flow of heat through a fluid by movement of the fluid itself.
Heat radiation is a transfer of heat that requires a material medium.
The rate of heat flow $H$ is the amount of heat energy transferred per unit time.
A blackbody is an object which can absorb all the electromagnetic radiations falling upon it.
The thermal efficiency of a heat engine is defined as the ratio of work input to heat output.
The Carnot engine was described by Nicolas Leonard Sadi Carnot in 1824.
In a reversible process, every point along the path on the $p-V$ diagram is in an equilibrium state.
During the isothermal expansion of a Carnot cycle, the gas absorbs heat from the high temperature reservoir.
No engine operating between two heat reservoirs can be more efficient than a Carnot engine operating between the same reservoirs.
Thermal conductivity is a measure of the ability of a material to conduct heat.
In a diesel engine cycle, the fuel injector is located near the air intake.
The Carnot cycle is considered a hypothetical engine cycle.
Gasoline engines typically operate between lower temperatures than diesel engines.
Isothermal processes in the Carnot cycle occur at constant temperatures.
The surroundings do work on the gas during the isothermal compression step.
The driving force in a reversible process must be large enough to maintain a state of equilibrium.
Heat conduction involves the movement of the individual parts of a medium as a whole.
The working substance of a Carnot engine is a real gas.
Efficiency of a heat engine can reach 100% theoretically.
The Carnot engine provides an upper limit for the efficiency of all real engines.
Absolute temperature is used when calculating the maximum theoretical efficiency.
The Carnot cycle is a reversible cycle.
